---
title: "Research Infrastructures<br>for the Digital Humanities"
subtitle: "Huma-Num · DARIAH · EOSC"
author: "Julien Rabaud"
institute: |
  Applied Mathematics Research Library · UPPA  
  Pôle Numérique · Research Data Management
date: 2026-03-16
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, slides.scss]
    slide-number: c/t
    footer: "UPPA · ED 481 SSH · CHORAL (UNITA) · Julien Rabaud · 16 march 2026"
    transition: slide
    background-transition: fade
    controls: true
    progress: true
    history: true
    smaller: false
    scrollable: true
    highlight-style: github
    lang: en
    embed-resources: true
    chalkboard: false
    mermaid:
      theme: neutral
---

# Introduction {background-color="#003D7A"}

## What are Research Infrastructures? {.center}

> Research Infrastructures (RIs) are **facilities, resources, and services** that are used by the research community to conduct research and foster innovation.
> 
> — *European Commission*

::: {.incremental}
- They can be physical (*laboratories, telescopes*) or **digital** (*platforms, data repositories, services*)
- For the humanities, **digital RIs** provide tools to produce, share, and preserve research data
- They are increasingly organised at **European level**, under the ESFRI roadmap
:::

## Why should doctoral students care? {.smaller}

::: {.incremental}
- Funding agencies (ANR, ERC, Horizon Europe) **require** Data Management Plans
- **Open access** and **open data** are increasingly mandatory for publicly-funded research
- These infrastructures provide **free tools** you may already need — or will need
- They connect you to **European research communities** — especially valuable within UNITA
- They offer **training, workshops, and summer schools**
- Your data deposited through them will still be **findable in 20 years**
:::

::: {.fragment}
::: {.callout-tip appearance="minimal"}
Think of research infrastructures as the **library system** of digital research: you don't build it yourself, but knowing how to use it makes all the difference.
:::
:::

## Three interconnected infrastructures

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="33%"}
![](https://www.huma-num.fr/wp-content/uploads/2021/01/HN_LOGO_COULEUR-RVB_Logo-scaled.jpg){height="70px" fig-alt="Huma-Num"}

**Huma-Num**  
French national infrastructure  
*Your starting point*
:::
::: {.column width="33%"}
![](https://www.dariah.eu/wp-content/uploads/2019/09/dariah_logo_color.png){height="70px" fig-alt="DARIAH"}

**DARIAH-EU**  
Pan-European network  
*22 member states*
:::
::: {.column width="33%"}
![](https://www.eosc.eu/sites/default/files/inline-images/EOSC_logo_tag_0.png){height="70px" fig-alt="EOSC"}

**EOSC**  
European Open Science Cloud  
*The bigger picture*
:::
:::

# Part 1 · Huma-Num {background-color="#981b3e"}

## What is Huma-Num?

**Huma-Num** is a French *Très Grande Infrastructure de Recherche* (TGIR) dedicated to digital humanities.

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
A **TGIR** (*Very Large Research Infrastructure*) is a national designation by the French Ministry of Higher Education and Research. Huma-Num is supported and hosted by **CNRS** (UAR 3598).
:::

::: {.incremental}
- Created in **2013** from TGE Adonis (est. 2006)
- Provides **tools, services, and community infrastructure** for SSH researchers
- France's national node for **DARIAH-EU** (*DARIAH-FR*)
- France is the **host country** of the DARIAH-ERIC secretariat
:::

## The Data Lifecycle

![](https://www.huma-num.fr/wp-content/uploads/2022/05/Schema-de-la-vie-des-donnees-HN-2023-1024x205.png){fig-alt="Huma-Num data lifecycle" width="100%"}

**Collect → Process → Store → Share**

From raw data to open, reusable, FAIR research outputs — Huma-Num supports every stage.

## FAIR Data Principles

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="25%"}
### 🔍 F
**Findable**  
Persistent identifiers (DOI, ARK) + rich metadata
:::
::: {.column width="25%"}
### 🔓 A
**Accessible**  
Open protocols, clear access conditions
:::
::: {.column width="25%"}
### 🔗 I
**Interoperable**  
Shared vocabularies, ontologies, open formats
:::
::: {.column width="25%"}
### ♻️ R
**Reusable**  
Clear licences, provenance documentation
:::
:::

::: {.fragment}
→ Wilkinson et al. (2016), *Scientific Data* | [go-fair.org](https://www.go-fair.org)
:::

## Key Services {.smaller}

| Service | What it does | Link |
|---------|-------------|------|
| **NAKALA** | Deposit & publish FAIR research data (ARK identifiers) | [nakala.fr](https://nakala.fr) |
| **Isidore** | Discover SSH publications & datasets | [isidore.science](https://isidore.science) |
| **Stylo** | Semantic text editor for academic writing | [stylo.huma-num.fr](https://stylo.huma-num.fr) |
| **Huma-Num Box** | Collaborative cloud storage (Nextcloud) | [box.huma-num.fr](https://box.huma-num.fr) |
| **Virtual Machines** | On-demand computing environments for projects | [huma-num.fr/services](https://www.huma-num.fr/les-services-et-les-outils/) |

## Thematic Consortiums {.smaller}

Huma-Num structures its community through **thematic consortiums** — collaborative networks that share methodologies, produce guides, organise training, and build data repositories.

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
🖼 **PICTORIA** — Images & visual sources  
📦 **ARIANE** — Archives science  
🏛 **3D-SHS** — 3D modelling  
🎭 **CANEVAS** — Performing arts  
🎵 **MUSICA2** — Musicology  
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
⚗️ **MASA+** — Archaeological data  
🗣 **CORLI** — Spoken language corpora  
🧬 **SOLIFE** — Biographical data  
🗺 **PTM** — Photogrammetry  
🏷 **DISTAM** — Heraldic & stamp data  
:::
:::

::: {.fragment .callout-tip appearance="minimal"}
**For doctoral students:** one or more consortiums may be directly relevant to your dissertation. Connecting early gives access to community expertise, methodological guides, and training events.
:::

# Part 2 · DARIAH-EU {background-color="#003D7A"}

## What is DARIAH?

**DARIAH** = *Digital Research Infrastructure for the Arts and Humanities*

::: {.incremental}
- European **Research Infrastructure Consortium (ERIC)** since August **2014**
- **22 Member States** + cooperating partners
- Host country: **France** (secretariat in Paris, managed by Huma-Num)
- Mission: *empowering arts & humanities research communities with digital methods to create, connect, and share knowledge about culture and society*
- Part of the **ESFRI Roadmap** of strategic European research infrastructures
:::

## Four Strategic Pillars

```{=html}
<div style="display:grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; gap: 1rem; margin-top:1rem;">
  <div style="background:rgba(255,255,255,0.12); border-left:4px solid #f5c518; padding:1rem; border-radius:6px;">
    <strong>🔬 Research</strong><br>
    Digital methods for arts & humanities — text analysis, data modelling, digital editions
  </div>
  <div style="background:rgba(255,255,255,0.12); border-left:4px solid #f5c518; padding:1rem; border-radius:6px;">
    <strong>📚 Education & Training</strong><br>
    Building digital skills across the community — DARIAH Campus, summer schools
  </div>
  <div style="background:rgba(255,255,255,0.12); border-left:4px solid #f5c518; padding:1rem; border-radius:6px;">
    <strong>📢 Advocacy</strong><br>
    Promoting open science and digital humanities in European research policy
  </div>
  <div style="background:rgba(255,255,255,0.12); border-left:4px solid #f5c518; padding:1rem; border-radius:6px;">
    <strong>🏗️ Infrastructure</strong><br>
    Developing and maintaining shared technical resources and interoperable services
  </div>
</div>
```

## Tools and Platforms

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**DARIAH Campus**  
Open training platform — courses, modules, and resources for digital arts and humanities  
🔗 [campus.dariah.eu](https://campus.dariah.eu)

**SSH Open Marketplace**  
Discover tools, workflows, datasets, and training materials for SSH research  
🔗 [marketplace.sshopencloud.eu](https://marketplace.sshopencloud.eu)
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Working Groups**  
Thematic communities open to all researchers at member institutions:

- Text & Data Analytics
- Digital Methods (DiMPO)
- Ethics & Legality (ELDAH)
- Women Writers in History
- DARIAH Teach

🔗 [dariah.eu/working-groups](https://www.dariah.eu/activities/working-groups/)
:::
:::

## DARIAH and UNITA Countries

UNITA partner countries are connected to DARIAH in various capacities:

| Country | DARIAH status |
|---------|--------------|
| 🇫🇷 France | Founding member · **host country** |
| 🇮🇹 Italy | Founding member |
| 🇪🇸 Spain | Member |
| 🇵🇹 Portugal | Member |
| 🇷🇴 Romania | Member |
| 🇨🇭 Switzerland | Cooperating partner (DARIAH-CH in progress) |
| 🇺🇦 Ukraine | Cooperating partner |

::: {.fragment}
→ Every UNITA partner country has a national node or cooperating structure. You are already connected.
:::

# Part 3 · EOSC & the European Landscape {background-color="#2c7a4b"}

## What is EOSC?

The **European Open Science Cloud** is a flagship European Commission initiative: a federated environment for storing, sharing, and reusing **research data across all disciplines**.

::: {.incremental}
- Launched as a concept in **2016**, operational since **2021**
- Brings together **research infrastructures, data repositories, and service providers** from across Europe
- Governed by the [EOSC Association](https://eosc.eu) — a legal entity grouping research organisations
- For the humanities: DARIAH and CLARIN are **EOSC members** and have integrated their services
- Connects to national data strategies (Plan national pour la science ouverte, etc.)
:::

## EOSC for Humanities Researchers

::: {.incremental}
- **Discoverability**: your data deposited on NAKALA or via DARIAH/CLARIN nodes becomes searchable through the EOSC portal
- **Cross-disciplinary connections**: find corpora, tools, and datasets alongside sciences
- **Persistent identifiers**: EOSC promotes PID-based data (DOI, ARK, ORCID) — what Huma-Num already does
- **FAIR as a requirement**: Horizon Europe grants increasingly require EOSC-compatible, FAIR data outputs
:::

::: {.fragment .callout-note appearance="minimal"}
Think of EOSC as the **European commons** for research data — Huma-Num, DARIAH, and CLARIN are France and the humanities' **contribution** to that commons.
:::

## The SSH Open Marketplace

A shared discovery portal co-developed by **DARIAH**, **CLARIN**, and **CESSDA** — and integrated into EOSC.

What you can find:

| Category | Examples |
|----------|---------|
| **Tools & Services** | Annotation tools, text editors, OCR |
| **Training materials** | Tutorials, slides, course modules |
| **Datasets** | Corpora, archives, digitised collections |
| **Workflows** | Documented research method descriptions |

🔗 [marketplace.sshopencloud.eu](https://marketplace.sshopencloud.eu)

## A Word on CLARIN

**CLARIN ERIC** (*Common Language Resources and Technology Infrastructure*) is the third major SSH European infrastructure — focused on **language data, NLP tools, and text analysis**.

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
Relevant even beyond linguistics:

- **Historical newspaper corpora** — Named Entity Recognition
- **Literary studies** — stylometric analysis, text encoding (TEI)
- **Oral sources** — spoken language corpora (via CORLI / Huma-Num)
:::
::: {.column width="45%"}
Key tools:

- **VLO** — discover language resources
- **Switchboard** — find NLP tools for your text
- **WebLicht** — compose NLP workflows

🔗 [clarin.eu](https://www.clarin.eu)
:::
:::

## The Full Picture

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
    ESFRI["🇪🇺 ESFRI Roadmap"]
    EOSC["EOSC\nEuropean Open\nScience Cloud"]
    DARIAH["DARIAH-EU\nARTS & HUMANITIES"]
    CLARIN["CLARIN ERIC\nLANGUAGE"]
    MARKET["SSH Open\nMarketplace"]
    HN["Huma-Num 🇫🇷"]
    DARIAH_FR["DARIAH-FR"]
    CLARIN_FR["CLARIN-FR"]
    UPPA["UPPA · UNITA"]

    ESFRI --> DARIAH
    ESFRI --> CLARIN
    DARIAH <--> EOSC
    CLARIN <--> EOSC
    DARIAH <--> CLARIN
    DARIAH --> MARKET
    CLARIN --> MARKET
    HN --> DARIAH_FR --> DARIAH
    HN --> CLARIN_FR --> CLARIN
    UPPA --> HN
    UPPA --> DARIAH
```

# What's Next For You? {background-color="#003D7A"}

## Practical Starting Points

::: {.incremental}
1. 📂 **Deposit your data** → [NAKALA](https://nakala.fr) — FAIR, persistent, citable
2. 🔍 **Find tools & workflows** → [SSH Marketplace](https://marketplace.sshopencloud.eu)
3. 📖 **Train yourself** → [DARIAH Campus](https://campus.dariah.eu)
4. 🤝 **Join a consortium** → [Huma-Num consortiums](https://www.huma-num.fr/les-consortiums-scientifiques/)
5. 🌐 **Join a working group** → [DARIAH working groups](https://www.dariah.eu/activities/working-groups/)
6. 📝 **Write your DMP** → [DMP OPIDoR](https://dmp.opidor.fr) with consortium guidance
:::

::: {.fragment .callout-note appearance="minimal"}
All these services are **free** for researchers affiliated with European universities and research institutions.
:::

## Key Links

| Resource | URL |
|---------|-----|
| Huma-Num | [huma-num.fr](https://www.huma-num.fr) |
| NAKALA | [nakala.fr](https://nakala.fr) |
| Isidore | [isidore.science](https://isidore.science) |
| DARIAH | [dariah.eu](https://www.dariah.eu) |
| DARIAH Campus | [campus.dariah.eu](https://campus.dariah.eu) |
| CLARIN | [clarin.eu](https://www.clarin.eu) |
| SSH Marketplace | [marketplace.sshopencloud.eu](https://marketplace.sshopencloud.eu) |
| EOSC | [eosc.eu](https://eosc.eu) |

## Thank you {.center background-color="#981b3e"}

**Questions?**

<br>

Julien Rabaud  
Applied Mathematics Research Library · UPPA  
Pôle Numérique · Research Data Management

📧 [julien.rabaud\@univ-pau.fr](mailto:julien.rabaud@univ-pau.fr)  
🔗 Companion site: *[your URL here]*
